ABSTRACT


The Lost and Found Items Management System is a digital solution designed to improve the process of reporting, managing, and recovering misplaced belongings in institutions, workplaces, and public areas. Traditional lost-and-found systems rely on manual registers or notice boards, which are inefficient, prone to error, and lack real-time accessibility. To overcome these challenges, this project leverage Flask (Python) as the backend framework along with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for an intuitive frontend interface.
The system provides essential functionalities such as user authentication (login and registration), posting lost or found items with images, browsing item listings, and basic communication between users. A structured database ensures that all records are securely stored and easily, reducing delays  and increasing efficiency.
By offering a centralized and user-friendly platform, the application encourages community participation and significantly increases the chances of recovering lost belongings. It demonstrates  how simple web technologies can address real-world problems, while also providing a foundation for future enhancements such as AI-powered search, cloud deployment, and mobile app integration.

Keywords:
Lost and Found System, Web Application, Flask, Python, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, User Authentication, Database Management, Item Recovery, Information Management.

Chapter: 1

INTRODUCTION



In our everyday lives, losing personal belongings is an experience that almost every individual goes through at some point. Whether it is misplacing a wallet, forgetting a mobile phone in a classroom, leaving a bag on public transport, or dropping identity cards and accessories in crowded areas, such incidents are very common and create significant inconvenience, frustration, and even financial burden. At the same time, people frequently come across items that are unattended or abandoned, but in most cases, they do not have a proper channel through which to report the discovery or return the object to its rightful owner. The lack of structured communication between the person who has lost an item and the one who has found it has long been a problem in both small and large communities. Traditionally, institutions and organizations have relied on manual methods such as notice boards, handwritten registers, or word-of-mouth communication to manage lost and found activities. While these methods have served their purpose in small settings, they are inherently inefficient, time-consuming, prone to errors, and lack the broader reach necessary in today’s interconnected world.
The problem becomes even more complicated in large-scale environments such as universities, shopping malls, airports, and corporate campuses where thousands of individuals move in and out daily. In such spaces, lost belongings can easily end up far from their original location, and without a structured process, reuniting them with their owners becomes highly unlikely. This reality highlights the importance of having a centralized, digital system that can streamline the reporting and recovery process. With the advancement of technology and the increasing reliance on digital solutions for everyday problems, a web-based platform for lost and found management emerges as an ideal solution.

By moving this process online, people are provided with a convenient, accessible, and reliable way of reporting lost belongings, posting found items, and searching for possible matches in real time. The idea is to reduce human effort, save time, improve record-keeping, and increase the chances of items being returned to their owners.
The project “Lost and Found Web Application” was conceptualized and developed to address these challenges. It is a centralized web-based system designed to connect individuals who have lost items with those who have found them. The platform allows users to register and log in securely, post details about items they have lost or discovered, upload images for better identification, and search through existing records. In addition, it integrates a messaging system or inbox feature, enabling direct communication between the finder and the owner. This ensures that the process is not just about posting information but also about facilitating a meaningful and secure interaction that can lead to the successful return of belongings. By bringing together essential features like registration, posting, searching, filtering, communication, and database management, the application provides a comprehensive solution to the lost and found problem.
The project is motivated by several practical and social factors. Losing items does not only cause material inconvenience but also creates psychological stress and, in many cases, financial costs. Replacing documents such as ID cards, driving licenses, or academic certificates is not only time-consuming but also requires bureaucratic processes. Similarly, gadgets like mobile phones, laptops, or accessories carry both monetary and emotional value, making their loss deeply frustrating. On the other hand, finding and returning an item contributes to a sense of community and trust. A digital platform that facilitates this process therefore does more than solve a logistical issue; it promotes ethical behavior, social responsibility, and goodwill among individuals.
Technically, the system is built using Python’s Flask framework for the backend, which is lightweight, scalable, and well-suited for building web applications. The frontend is developed using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript,

which together create an intuitive, user-friendly interface. Flask integrates seamlessly with the Jinja2 template engine, which allows dynamic rendering of data from the backend into the frontend templates. A relational database (such as SQLite or MySQL) is employed to store and manage records of users, lost items, found items, and conversations. The use of a structured database ensures reliable storage, easy retrieval, and the ability to perform queries that enable features such as search and filtering. Static resources such as images, CSS stylesheets, and JavaScript scripts are organized in dedicated folders, ensuring that the project follows good software engineering practices. This modular design makes the system not only functional but also maintainable and extendable for future improvements.
In conclusion, the Lost and Found web application is a holistic solution to a very common but often poorly managed problem. By combining secure user management, structured posting of lost and found items, image uploads, efficient search mechanisms, direct communication, and organized database management, the system provides a reliable, transparent, and user- friendly platform. It addresses not only the practical challenges of locating and recovering lost belongings but also the social and psychological dimensions by fostering trust, responsibility, and community participation. Built with modern web technologies, it is scalable, maintainable, and adaptable for future advancements. Thus, this project exemplifies how software engineering and web development can be applied to create systems with real-world relevance, making everyday life more convenient, organized, and connected.



Chapter: 2

LITERATURE SURVEY



A literature survey helps understand existing solutions, identify gaps, and position the current project more clearly. Below are various systems, research papers, and practical initiatives related to Lost & Found applications, along with their advantages, limitations, and lessons learned.
Background and Motivation
The idea for a Lost and Found web application originates from practical day-to-day challenges faced in schools, universities, offices, and public spaces. In many institutions, there is often no proper system in place to report or track missing items. Even if records are maintained, they are usually in handwritten registers which are prone to human error, mismanagement, and lack accessibility. Physical notice boards can be helpful but are often overlooked or may not reach the concerned individuals in time.
Moreover, in large campuses such as universities, shopping malls, airports, or corporate offices, the problem is magnified due to the scale of people involved. Items may be lost in one corner of the campus and found in another, with no efficient way of bridging the gap. This inspired the development of a dedicated web-based solution that can bridge communication gaps between the person who lost an item and the one who found it.
The rise of cloud computing, mobile devices, and online services has created an environment where web applications are the most effective way of solving community problems. By moving the lost-and-found process online, it ensures wider reach, easy accessibility, quick updates, and reliable storage of records. Users can log in from anywhere, upload details about lost or found items, and instantly communicate with other users. This level of efficiency is impossible

to achieve through manual systems.
Problem Statement
The major problem being addressed by this project is the lack of a structured, reliable, and user-friendly system for reporting and recovering lost items. Current manual methods have several limitations:
Inefficiency – Time-consuming, requiring individuals to visit notice boards or contact offices repeatedly.
Limited visibility – Information does not reach a wider audience.
Data loss – Physical registers can be misplaced, damaged, or poorly maintained.
Lack of authentication – Manual systems may allow misuse, such as false claims on lost property.
No search functionality – It is extremely difficult to filter and find items based on specific details.
Thus, a solution that overcomes these limitations by integrating modern database systems, user authentication, and efficient communication tools is highly essential.

Objectives of the Project
The Lost and Found Web Application has the following key objectives:
To develop a centralized digital platform for reporting lost and found items.
To allow users to register and log in securely for posting details.
To provide options for users to upload item details and images for easy identification.
To implement a search and filter system that allows users to quickly find relevant items.
To create a communication channel (such as inbox or messaging) between the finder and the owner.
To maintain a database-driven record system that can be updated, managed, and retrieved efficiently.

To ensure the application is user-friendly, accessible, and can handle real- time updates.
By meeting these objectives, the project aims to deliver a reliable, transparent, and easy-to-use platform for managing lost and found records.
Technologies Used
The project is built using a combination of modern web development tools and frameworks:
Backend: Python with Flask framework – lightweight and efficient for web apps.
Frontend: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript – for user interfaces, forms, and interactive components.
Templates: Jinja2 template engine – to integrate backend logic with frontend design.
Database: Likely SQLite or MySQL – for storing user details, lost/found records, and messages.
Other Tools: Bootstrap or custom CSS for responsive design, along with JavaScript for interactivity.
This technology stack was chosen for its simplicity, scalability, and suitability for academic and real-world projects. Flask, being a micro-framework, allows flexibility while maintaining clarity and ease of use.
Features of the System
The Lost and Found application incorporates several core features, including:
User Registration and Login – Secure authentication for users.
Dashboard – Personalized area for users to manage their posts and interactions.
Add Lost/Found Item – Form to submit item details, category, description, and image.
View Items – Users can browse all posted items with filters and search options.

Search Functionality – Keyword and category-based search for quick access.
Messaging/Inbox – Communication between finder and owner.
Profile Management – Users can update their information.
Responsive UI – Accessible across devices with easy navigation.
These features make the application interactive, practical, and effective in addressing the lost and found problem.

Significance of the Project
The significance of this project extends across multiple domains:
Educational institutions – Students and staff can easily report and recover items.
Workplaces – Employees can track lost assets like ID cards or gadgets.
Public spaces – Airports, malls, and transport hubs can integrate the system for visitors.
Community use – General public can use it as a community-driven lost and found service.
By digitizing the process, the project contributes to time savings, improved reliability, and enhanced community trust.

Scope of the Project
The scope of this project is quite broad. While currently designed as a web application, the same concept can be expanded to include:
Mobile app integration – For quick and on-the-go reporting.
Advanced search filters – Based on location, date, or category.
Admin dashboard – For institutions to verify and manage items.
AI-powered matching – Automated matching between lost and found entries.
Scalability – Deployment on cloud servers for larger communities.
Thus, the project provides a foundation for developing an advanced digital lost

and found system with future improvements.
Expected Outcomes
The expected outcomes of this project include:
A fully functional web application with lost and found item management.
Improved chances of reuniting people with their belongings.
Reduced dependency on manual systems.
Better record-keeping and retrieval.
Positive user experience with simple, intuitive design.


This project, therefore, serves as an excellent example of how software engineering principles and web development skills can be applied to design systems that have practical and social relevance. It lays the groundwork for future enhancements like mobile integration, cloud deployment, and intelligent matching, ensuring that the problem of lost and found items is addressed with maximum efficiency and reliability.



Chapter: 3

METHODOLOGY


The methodology of the Lost and Found Web Application focuses on the systematic approach followed to analyze the problem, gather requirements, design the system, implement functionalities, test modules, and finally evaluate results. The system was developed using the Flask web framework in Python for backend functionality, combined with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for the frontend, and a relational database for storing records of users and items. The overall goal of the methodology is to provide a structured digital solution to connect individuals who lose their belongings with those who find them.

PROJECT REQURIMENTS
The project requirements are divided into two categories:
Functional Requirements:
User registration and login for secure access.
Ability to add a lost item with details (item name, category, description, date, and image).
Ability to add a found item with details and upload evidence (image).
Search and filter features to quickly find items.
Inbox/messaging feature for direct communication between finder and owner.
Dashboard to manage user activity and interactions.
Profile management for updating user details.
Secure logout functionality.
Non-Functional Requirements:
User-friendly interface with responsive design.
Secure handling of user credentials.

Efficient database for storing item and user details.
Scalability to support more users in larger institutions.
Compatibility with major web browsers.
Hardware Requirements:
Minimum: Intel i3 Processor, 4 GB RAM, 500 GB HDD.
Recommended: Intel i5/i7, 8 GB RAM, 1 TB HDD/SSD.
Software Requirements:
Operating System: Windows/Linux/MacOS.
Backend: Python (Flask framework).
Frontend: HTML, CSS, JavaScript.
Database: SQLite/MySQL.
Tools: VS Code, Flask libraries, pip package manager, browser for testing.

SYSTEM DESIGN
The system follows a three-tier architecture:
Presentation Layer (Frontend):
HTML templates such as index.html, login.html, register.html, dashboard.html.
CSS files for styling (login.css, dashboard.css, style.css).
JavaScript (script.js) for validation and interactivity.
Application Layer (Backend):
Implemented using Flask (app.py).
Handles user authentication, routes (login, register, add item, view items).
Connects frontend templates to the database.
Manages messaging and profile updates.
Data Layer (Database):
Stores user details, lost item entries, found item entries, conversations.

Ensures secure retrieval and filtering.
Enables query-based search functions.
Flow of the system:
User → Frontend interface → Flask routes in backend → Database operations → Dynamic rendering on frontend.

WORKING WEB APPLICATION
The web application workflow can be summarized as:
User Registration/Login:
New users sign up with name, email, password.
Existing users log in securely.
Dashboard:
After login, users access the dashboard where they can add items, view items, or check messages.
Posting Lost/Found Items:
Users select “Lost” or “Found,” fill details, and upload images.
Data is stored in the database and displayed to all users.
Viewing/Search Items:
Users search items by category, keyword, or date.
Matches are displayed with item details and contact options.
Inbox/Conversation:
If a user finds a matching post, they can contact the other party through the messaging feature.
Conversations are stored securely for future reference.
Profile Management:
Users can update their details and manage their activity history.
Logout:
Secure logout ends the session.

INFORMATION FLOW REPRESENTATION


The user flow describes the steps a user takes to interact with the lost and found web application. This flow is designed to be intuitive and efficient, ensuring users can easily perform actions such as listing lost or found items, searching for items, and contacting other users.
User Registration and Authentication
Application: Users navigate to the web application’s homepage.
Register/Login: New users can register by providing basic information or by using OAuth providers like Google or Facebook for quick sign-up. Returning users can log
in using their credentials or OAuth accounts.
Authentication: NextAuth manage the authentication process, ensuring secure access.
Dashboard
Welcome Screen: Upon successful login, users are directed to the dashboard where they can see an overview of their activities and available functionalities.
Navigation Options: The dashboard provides easy navigation to list an item, search for items, and view user profile settings.
Listing a Lost or Found Item
Select Listing Type: Users choose whether they want to create a ‘Lost’ or ‘Found’ item listing.
Enter Item Details: Users fill out a form with details including the item title, description, category, date, and contact information.
Upload Images: Users have the option to upload images of the item to enhance the listing.
Submit Listing: Users submit the form, and the item is added to the

MongoDB database through Prisma ORM. The listing becomes visible to other users searching for items.

Searching for Lost or Found Items
Access Search Page: Users navigate to the search page from the dashboard.
Enter Search Criteria: Users enter keywords, select categories (lost or found), and apply filters to narrow down the search results.
View	Results: The	application retrieves	matching	items	from the database and displays them in a user-friendly format.
Item Details: User can click on an item to view

Fig-1: User Approach
Contacting Users
View Item Details: On the item details page, users can view the contact information provided by the listing creator.
Initiate Contact: Users can contact the listing creator directly via phone or email to discuss the lost or found item.
Communication: The application encourages direct communication to expedite the process of reuniting lost items with their owners.


Profile Management
Access Profile Settings: Users can access their profile settings from the dashboard.
Update Information: Users can update their personal information, contact details, and profile picture.
Manage Listings: Users can view, edit, or delete their own listings from the profile settings.
Logout
Secure Logout: Users can securely log out of their account from any page within the application.
The user flow is designed to ensure a seamless and intuitive experience, guiding users through the essential steps of registering, listing items, searching for items, and making contact with others.

Fig-2: Flow of Application

How to Implement the Lost and Found Project

GitHub Link - https://github.com/TrioCoders-892/LostandFound/
Prerequisites
Python 3 installed
Internet connection for MongoDB Atlas (or local MongoDB setup)
Required Python packages: Flask, Flask-PyMongo, Werkzeug
Project Setup
Copy or clone the entire project folder to the target system.
Install Dependencies
Open a terminal in the project directory.
Run:
pip install flask flask-pymongo werkzeug
Configure Database
Use the provided MongoDB Atlas URI in app.py, or update it with your own credentials.
Ensure MongoDB is accessible from the new system.
Run the Application
In the terminal, execute:
python app.py
The application will start on http://127.0.0.1:5000/.
Access and Use
Open a web browser and go to http://127.0.0.1:5000/.
Register, log in, and use all features as intended

APPLICATIONS

Here are some key applications:
Educational Institutions (Schools, Colleges, Universities)
Students and staff can report misplaced books, ID cards, electronic devices, or personal belongings.
Reduces manual record-keeping by security or administration offices.
Corporate Offices & Workplaces
Employees can log found items (e.g., access cards, laptops, chargers).
Helps HR/admin track and return items quickly.
Public Places (Parks, Libraries, Auditoriums)
Visitors can register lost or found items, ensuring belongings are returned to rightful owners.
Events & Conferences
Organizers can use the platform to manage lost belongings during large events, exhibitions, or fairs.
Prevents confusion and helps attendees reclaim items faster.
Hostels & Housing Societies
Residents can report misplaced keys, clothes, or packages.
Encourages community cooperation in maintaining order.
Transport Facilities (Bus stands, Railway stations, Airports)
Travelers can report found luggage or missing items.
Staff can update entries in real time for easy retrieval.
Shopping Malls & Supermarkets
Shoppers can check if their lost items were found by others.
Mall management can track and return items systematically.
Clubs & Gyms
Helps members reclaim personal items like water bottles, headphones, or accessories.

Digital Communities
Can be extended for online lost & found (e.g., lost digital files, misplaced virtual event links).



ADVANTAGES


The Lost & Found web application saves time, increases efficiency, improves communication, and provides a reliable way to recover lost items. It eliminates the traditional manual process of maintaining registers or notice boards, making the entire system more organized and user-friendly. By allowing users to report, search, and track items in real time, the application ensures faster recovery and reduces stress for individual who misplace their belongings.
These are the some important Advantages:
Easy Accessibility
Users can access the platform anytime, anywhere via a browser.
Eliminates the need to physically visit an office or notice board.
Centralized System
All lost and found records are stored in one place.
Reduces confusion and duplication compared to manual registers.
Reporting
Users can instantly report a lost or found item.
Speeds up the process of locating owners and returning belongings.
Improved Communication
Built-in messaging/contact features help users connect directly.
Prevents delays caused by third-party mediators.
Image Support

Users can upload photos of items, making identification more accurate.
Increases the chances of rightful ownership verification.
Time-Saving
Reduces the time spent	searching through notice boards, offices, or asking people manually.
Owners can quickly search the database instead of waiting.
Scalability
Can be used in schools, universities, offices, malls, airports, and events.
Handles large numbers of users and items efficiently.
User-Friendly Interface
Simple design makes it easy for anyone (students, staff, employees, or the public) to navigate without technical knowledge.
Reduced Risk of Permanent Loss
With faster reporting and better communication, the chances of items being permanently lost are minimized.
Ownership Verification
Descriptions,	locations,	and	images	help	confirm	rightful ownership, reducing fraudulent claims.
Cost-Effective
Saves printing costs, manpower, and time compared to manual systems.
Enhanced Security
User authentication prevents outsiders from spamming or posting fake information.

DISADVANTAGES
Disadvantages and Challenges of Lost and Found Items:

Internet Dependency
Since the platform is web-based, users must have stable internet access. In areas with poor connectivity, the system may be less effective.
Limited Verification of Ownership
There is no foolproof process to confirm that the person claiming a lost item is the rightful owner, which could lead to disputes or misuse.
Risk of False Data
The system relies on user input. Users may upload false, duplicate, or misleading information, which can reduce the reliability of the database.
Technical Familiarity Required
Although the interface is simple, users must still know how to register, log in, and upload details, which may be difficult for people with very limited digital literacy.
Maintenance and Security Challenges
Continuous monitoring is required to ensure that the database is secure, free from spam entries, and protected against hacking or data theft.
Scalability Concerns
With increasing users and records, the system may require more resources (server space, stronger database management), which can increase costs and complexity.
No Automatic Notifications
Currently, users need to log in and check the dashboard manually. Lack of automated alerts or push notifications may delay the recovery process.

Future Enhancement:


User Profile Management:
Allow users to edit their profile, add contact info, and view their activity history.
Admin Dashboard:
Add admin features for moderating items, managing users, and viewing analytics.
Notifications:
Send email or in-app notifications for new messages, item status changes, or matches.
Image Recognition:
Integrate AI to help match found items with lost reports using image similarity.
Search & Filters:
Advanced search by keywords, location, date, and item type.
Mobile-Friendly Design:
Make the UI fully responsive and consider building a mobile app.
Item Return Workflow:
Add a process for confirming item returns, with feedback and ratings.
Location Integration:
Use maps to show item locations and help users find items nearby.
Security & Privacy:
Add	two-factor	authentication,	better	password	policies,	and	privacy controls.
API Integration:
Provide REST APIs for third-party integrations or mobile clients.

RESULT
The developed Lost and Found Management System successfully provides a digital platform where users can easily report, search, and recover lost or found items within the community. The system integrates Flask (backend), MongoDB (database), and HTML/CSS templates (frontend) to create a secure and user-friendly environment.
User Registration & Login: Secure account creation and authentication with encrypted passwords.
Lost & Found Item Management: Users can add item details (title, description, location, block, status, and image), stored in MongoDB and displayed on the dashboard.
Search & Filter: Items can be searched using keywords, status, or location for quick identification.
Communication: Built-in messaging system enables direct contact between owners and finders.
Dashboard: Provides a clear view of all items with relevant details and real- time updates.
Testing Results:
Users could register and log in.
Items were added and displayed successfully.
Messaging between users worked properly.
Items could be marked as found and removed from the active list.
Overall Result: The project provides a centralized, efficient, and user- friendly platform for managing lost and found items.


Chapter: 4
CONCLUSION


The Lost and Found Management System effectively addresses the inefficiencies of traditional methods like handwritten logs and notice boards by offering a centralized, web-based platform for reporting and recovering lost items. Built using Flask, MongoDB, and HTML/CSS, the system ensures secure user authentication, intuitive item reporting, and real-time accessibility. Key features such as search and filter tools, dashboard visualization, and integrated messaging streamline the recovery process and foster trust among users.
Through secure registration and detailed item uploads—including descriptions, locations, and images—the platform enhances clarity and accountability. The messaging system enables direct communication between item owners and finders, facilitating safe and efficient handovers. Rigorous testing confirmed the system’s stability, responsiveness, and reliability, with features like flash messages and image uploads improving user experience.
This digital solution is especially impactful in community settings like universities and offices, where it reduces confusion, saves time, and promotes transparency. By minimizing reliance on physical records, it exemplifies how technology can solve everyday problems in sustainable ways. Future enhancements such as mobile integration, push notifications, and AI-based image recognition could further elevate its scalability and effectiveness. Overall, the system successfully bridges the gap between item loss and recovery, setting a strong foundation for digital community management.

ABSTRACT  
 
        In the rapidly evolving landscape of media and journalism, “ Facts Time ” emerges as a        
automated newspaper designed to deliver timely, accurate, and comprehensive news coverage with 
minimal human intervention. Leveraging advanced technologies such as artificial intelligence, natural 
language processing, and machine learning, Facts Time  automates the entire news production 
pipeline —from data gathering and verification to content generation and distribution.  
The core innovation behind Facts Time  lies in its ability to continuously scan vast data sources 
including news wires, social media, official statements, and public databases in real -time. This 
enables the system to identify trending topics, breaking news, and emerging stories across diverse 
domains like politics, economics, technology, and culture. Automated fact -checking algorithms 
enhance credibility by cross -referencing multiple sources and flagging inconsistencies to prevent the 
spread of misinformation.  
Using sophisticated language models, Facts Time  transforms raw data into coherent, engaging, and 
unbiased news articles tailored to various audience preferences. The system supports multilingual 
publishing, expanding its accessibility globally. Additionally, its modular architecture allows 
seamless int egration with digital platforms such as websites, mobile apps, and social media channels, 
ensuring broad dissemination and user engagement.  
By automating repetitive and time -sensitive tasks, Facts Time  significantly reduces operational costs 
and turnaround time, empowering news organizations to focus on investigative journalism and in -
depth analysis. It also democratizes access to news by offering affordable and scalable solutions for 
smaller publishers  and independent journalists.  
This abstract explores the technical foundations, operational workflow, and societal implications of 
Facts Time , highlighting its role in addressing challenges such as information overload, news fatigue, 
and bias in traditional media. As automated journalism continues to mature, Facts Time  exemplifies 
a forward -thinking approach that harnesses technology to enhance transparency, speed, and reliability 
in news delivery.

INTRODUCTION  
 
             
           The Facts Time  is an innovative, modern digital newspaper platform designed to bring 
users a seamless and engaging news reading experience. In today’s fast -paced world, where 
information is abundant yet fragmented, The Facts Time aims to deliver clarity, accessibility, a nd 
personalization to its audience through a well -structured interface and thoughtfully implemented 
features. By leveraging a combination of intuitive design, responsive layout, and secure user 
authentication, this platform embodi es the essence of an automated yet user -centric news source.  
        At its core, The Facts Time is structured around delivering categorized news content that spans 
several critical domains of everyday interest — Business, Politics, Environment, Crime, Movies, and 
Sports. This wide range of categories allows users t o tailor their news consumption according to their 
personal interests and priorities. The platform’s navigation is designed to be straightforward: a 
horizontal menu bar containing clearly labeled buttons enables users to switch between categories 
effortles sly. Each button employs a distinctive style that highlights the active category, ensuring users 
always have clear visual cues about their current selection. The rounded corners and minimalistic 
design of these buttons contribute to an overall aesthetic th at is modern, approachable, and easy on 
the eyes.  
      The platform’s header serves a dual role by both branding the site and managing user access. 
Featuring the name "The Facts Time" in large, uppercase typography, the header immediately 
establishes the identity of the platform. Positioned strategically  on the right side are the authentication 
controls — login and signup buttons — which encourage users to create or access their accounts. This 
positioning allows for quick and natural user engagement without disrupting the balance of the 
header’s design. O nce a user logs in, the interface dynamically replaces these buttons with a profile 
icon contained within a circular avatar. This avatar not only personalizes the experience but also 
provides a gateway to profile management options. The careful styling of this avatar, with subtle 
shadows, teal borders, and perfectly rounded edges, reflects the platform’s commitment to polished 
visuals and user -friendly interactions.  
       Below the header, a dynamically updated date display provides users with immediate temporal 
context. Displaying the current date prominently but unobtrusively reinforces the notion that the 
platform is up -to-date and relevant. This feature, while simple, plays an important psychological role 
by anchoring users in the present moment as they browse news stories.  
The main content area, which serves as the primary window for news articles, is presented within a 
softly translucent white container with rounded corners. This design choice creates a comfortable 
reading environment that helps reduce eye strain and visual ly separates content from the background. 
The container’s size is optimized for readability, centered on the page, and adaptable to various screen 
sizes, ensuring the platform is accessible across devices from desktop monitors to mobile phones. 
Within this  container, news articles are displayed with thoughtful typographic hierarchy — headlines 
are bold and prominent, metadata such as publication dates or authorship is subtly muted but clearly 
readable, and descriptions are formatted for clarity and ease of consumption. Images accompanying

 articles are rendered with a consistent style that maintains aspect ratio, uses object -fit properties, and 
features gentle rounding of corners to harmonize with the overall visual theme.  
The platform’s design philosophy is deeply rooted in enhancing user experience and accessibility. 
Interactive elements such as buttons have been crafted with generous touch targets and clear hover 
states, facilitating ease of navigation for all users, incl uding those on touch devices or with motor 
impairments. Furthermore, the color palette, dominated by calm teal hues contrasted with white and 
muted backgrounds, is carefully chosen to be both visually appealing and accessible to users with a

Authentication is a vital component of The Facts Time, enabling personalized interactions and secure

ample padding and rounded corners to create inviting, card -like forms. The forms themselves are

security standards  
Once logged in, users gain access to profile management features through a dedicated modal.                                               
This profile modal echoes the design language of the authentication forms, ensuring consistency and    
reducing cognitive load. Users can view and update profile details, change their profile picture, and manage 
other account settings within a scrollable container designed to accommodate varying amounts of  content

Overall, The Facts Time is more than just a news platform —it is a carefully crafted experience that 
harmonizes technology, design, and content to meet the evolving needs of today’s news consumers. 
By blending a modern aesthetic with robust functionality an d accessibility, it creates a trusted space 
for users to stay informed and connected. Every detail, from the typography and color scheme to the

to a unified , engaging, and reliable digital newspaper platform.  
Through its focus on user -centric design and thoughtful implementation of features, The Facts Time 
exemplifies the future of digital journalism, where accessibility meets elegance and information is 
delivered not just quickly, but meaningfully.

2.1 Background  and Motivation

shift has been driven by widespread internet availability, mobile device proliferation, and  
the demand for real -time updates. However, the explosion  
of online news sources has created a highly competitive environment where  users  
are bombarded with excessive amounts of information, often of varying credibility and  
relevance. Consequently, the challenge is no longer about access to news but about filtering, 
categorizing, and presenting news in a way that is meaningful, trustworthy.  
           The motivation behind “The Facts Time” stems from the need to address these challenges  
by designing a digital news platform that emphasizes clarity,  usability, and security.  
The project seeks to create an interface that organizes news articles  into well -defined categories, 
enabling users to easily find content that matches the ir interests. The interface is designed to be 
clean and modern, avoiding visual clutter and enhancing readability through the use of appropriate

3.2  SYSTEM  DESIGN  
1.Presentation  Layer  (Frontend):  
This is the layer that interacts directly with the user.  
• Built using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.  
• Provides login, registration, password reset, and profile management interfaces.  
• Uses forms for input and modals for feedback messages.  
• Displays the profile picture, name, email, and address of the logged -in user.  
• Calls JavaScript functions (like loginUser(), registerUser(), etc.) to process user actions.  
 
Department  of IT and AIDS  14  
 
 
 • Prevents right -click to restrict access to developer tools or saving images.  
• Uses event listeners like DOMContentLoaded to initialize the app when the page loads.  
2.Application  Layer  (Backend):  
       This layer handles the core logic and acts as a bridge between the frontend and 
backend (Supabase).  
• Written in JavaScript , using the Supabase  JavaScript SDK  for backend communication.  
• Handles user operations: sign up, login, logout, password reset, profile updates , and 
OAuth sign -in with Google . 
• Manages session state and automatically prompts login if the session expires.  
• Implements form validation and shows messages for user feedback using showMessage().  
• Checks for password recovery tokens in the URL and triggers the reset modal if detected.  
• Handles file uploads for profile pictures and updates the Supabase storage bucket.  
• Controls visibility and UI updates using DOM manipulation based on login state.  
 
3.Data Layer  (data base) : 
     This is managed by Supabase, providing database and authentication services.  
• Uses Supabase Auth for managing user sessions, password resets, and OAuth logins.  
• Stores user data like full_name, email, address_line1, and avatar_url in a PostgreSQL 
database under the profiles table.  
• Stores profile pictures in a Supabase storage bucket named avatars.  
• Enforces security using Row -Level Security (RLS) policies to control access to user data.  
• Returns data in response to API requests made via the frontend using the Supabase SDK.  
• Provides public URLs for uploaded images so they can be displayed on the frontend.  
 
Flow  of the system:  
• User  → Frontend  interface  → Flask  routes  in backend  → Database 
operations → Dynamic rendering on frontend.  
 
3.3  WORKING  WEB  APPLICATION  
 
1. User Registration  
  New users can sign up to "The Facts Time" by entering:  
• Full Name  
• Email Address  
• Address  
• Password & Confirm Password  
  Once the form is submitted:  
• The credentials are securely stored via Supabase Auth.  
• Additional profile information (e.g., address, full name) is saved in the profiles table.  
• A verification email is sent to confirm the user's identity before granting access.  
  This step ensures secure onboarding and prevents spam or bot accounts.  
 
  
Department  of IT and AIDS  15  
 
 
 2. User Login : 
Returning users can log in with their registered email and password. Upon successful authentication:  
• A secure session is created using Supabase.  
• The interface updates to display the latest news updates and user profile.  
• The system checks the profiles table to display personalized data.  
Invalid credentials trigger clear error messages, guiding the user to try again or reset their password.  
 
 3. Persistent Sessions & Auto Prompt  
The system regularly checks if a user is logged in:  
• If logged out, a login prompt appears, preventing access to premium or customized content.  
• If a valid session is active, the user remains logged in across sessions and tab changes.  
This ensures that users have uninterrupted access to personalized news feeds.  
 
 4. Logout Functionality  
Users can log out securely at any time:  
• Supabase clears the session on both client and server.  
• The interface reverts to the default, logged -out state.  
• A success message confirms the action, and the page reloads to show the latest public news.  
This prevents unauthorized access, especially on shared devices.  
 
 5. Forgot Password & Reset Flow  
If a user forgets their password, they can:  
1. Enter their email address to request a reset.  
2. Receive a secure password reset link via email.  
3. Reset the password using a dedicated modal within the app.  
Supabase handles token -based recovery securely, and users are immediately signed in after setting a 
new password.  
 
 6. User Profile Management  
Each user has access to a personal profile section, where they can:  
• View and update their full name and address.  
• Upload a profile picture stored in Supabase Storage.  
• See their email (read -only for security).  
This information is used to personalize the user experience and maintain engagement with localized 
or preferred news categories.  
 
 7. Security Highlights  
• Authentication is handled by Supabase, using JWT (JSON Web Tokens) for secure session 
handling.  
• RLS (Row -Level Security) policies ensure users can only access or modify their own data.  
• All passwords are encrypted and never exposed.  
• File uploads (e.g., profile pictures) are scoped per user to prevent access by others.

o If session is active → loads profile and shows news.

o User requests reset via email → Supabase sends reset link.  
o On visiting link → app detects recovery token in URL.  
o User resets password securely via modal.  
 
3. Database Interaction (Supabase Realtime DB)

o Fields: id, full_name , email, address_line1, avatar_url, username  
• On login, the app fetches and displays:  
o Name, email, address, and profile image.  
Flow:  
1. Insert profile data on sign -up 
2. Read profile data on login/session check  
3. Update profile data when user edits their info  
4. Upload and store avatar images via Supabase Storage bucket (avatars)

 3.9  DIS ADVANTAGES:  
 
1.Basic Authentication (No Real Security):  
•     Login and registration only store a flag in localStorage  without actual user validation or 
backend authentication — not secure for production.  
• No password encryption or user management; vulnerable to spoofing or unauthorized access 
if localStorage is manipulated.  
2.API Key Exposure:  
• The API key is exposed in client -side JavaScript, making it vulnerable to misuse or quota 
theft.  
• Should ideally use a backend proxy or server -side logic to hide API keys.  
3.Limited Pagination:  
• Only supports category switching and simple next/previous navigation on categories, but no 
pagination within a category for more news articles.  
Limited to max 6 articles per fetch, which might not be enough for heavy news consumers.  
4.Performance Concerns:  
• Fetching all articles on category change can lead to noticeable delays depending on network 
speed.  
• No caching mechanism to reduce redundant API calls.  
5.Dependence on External API:  
• Entire news content depends on GNews API availability and quota.  
• Free API restrictions limit article content and quantity.  
6.No User Personalization:  
• No option for users to customize topics, save favorite articles, or adjust news sources.  
• UI/UX could be more interactive.  
7.Accessibility and SEO:  
• Could benefit from better accessibility features (e.g., ARIA labels).  
• Content loaded dynamically may affect SEO if not server -side rendered  
8.No User Session Management:  
• Since login state is only stored in localStorage, it doesn’t handle user sessions securely or 
expire sessions automatically.  
9.No Backend Support:  
• All operations happen on the client side, so there’s no way to save user preferences, 
bookmarks, or personalized settings.

                                         CONCLUSION  
 
              The Web  News Portal  fulfills its objective of delivering categorized, real -time news in a 
secure and user -friendly environment. By leveraging Supabase  for full -stack backend capabilities 
and the GNews API  for dynamic content, the system minimizes manual effort and maximizes user 
engagement. The modular structure, reusable components, and real -time session awareness make 
it scalable and adaptable for future features such as bookmarking articles, comment sec tions, or 
integrating additional APIs. The project not only  demonstrates practical application of 
authentication and third -party API integration but also lays a strong foundation for educational or 
professional expansion into full -stack web development.  
 
           The Web News Portal  offers a modern web -based solution that simplifies the process of 
accessing categorized, real -time news while providing a personalized user experience. The project 
integrates Supabase  as the backend service for authentication, user management, and cloud 
storage, while GNews API  serves as the content provider for up -to-date news articles. The 
frontend, developed using HTML , CSS, and JavaScript , ensures the interface remains interactive 
and responsive across devices.  
 
             The implemented web application successfully integrates Supabase authentication and 
storage with a dynamic news dashboard. Users can register using email and password, log in, reset 
their password via email, and also authenticate using Google OAuth. Once l ogged in, users can 
view and update their profile, including uploading a profile picture stored in Supabase Storage. The 
system securely manages sessions, displays user -specific data, and dynamically adapts the UI 
depending on authentication s tate. The news section fetches category -based articles using the 
GNews API and displays them with relevant metadata and fallback mechanisms for missing 
content. Overall, the application delivers a full -stack solution with responsive design, real -time 
datab ase integration, and user personalization. The use of Supabase simplifies backend 
management, while the JavaScript frontend ensures interactive functionality. However, the app can 
be improved further by enhancing error handling, enforcing stricter security  measures, and 
modularizing the code for scalability. In conclusion, the project effectively demonstrates a modern 
web application with user authentication, profile management, and real -time news content, offering 
a solid foundation for expansion or deploy ment.
